mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs bringing the pointer-arithmetic start>=end relation closer to true, preserving sign-only-token seeds; I2S finds no literal to substitute, so its overhead hurts the loser.
one_line: pointer-arithmetic sign-only check; CMP_MAP gradient wins, I2S irrelevant

mechanism_summary: I2S substitution pulls the loser vpc corpus toward valid ICC header bytes including small version values <= 0x09, starving the branch that needs a byte greater than 0x09; plain havoc lands the high byte freely.
one_line: I2S steers loser toward valid small version byte, blocking >0x09 branch

mechanism_summary: I2S per-execution CmpLog overhead lowers throughput without providing comparison-solving traction on this non-literal gate; plain havoc explores IDAT-size fields reaching the post-stream extra-data state.
one_line: I2S overhead slows loser; havoc explores IDAT-size fields for extra-data path

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP prefix-distance gradient incrementally rewards script-tag bytes approaching the OLD_UYGHUR FOURCC constant; adding the I2S stage adds per-execution cost without improving over the gradient alone, causing throughput loss.
one_line: CMP_MAP gradient climbs toward OLD_UYGHUR script tag; I2S overhead blocks loser

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP gradient preserves inputs whose codepoint operand approaches 0x0E36 via Hamming-distance buckets, ratcheting toward exact match; vpc's added I2S substitution stage diverts mutation effort away from that gradient climb.
one_line: CMP_MAP gradient climbs codepoint toward 0x0E36; I2S addition diverts effort

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient steers winner toward non-space glyph structure; I2S in loser substitutes space-codepoint comparison operands (e.g. 0x0020) back into input, biasing loser corpus toward space-classified codepoints that block the false branch.
one_line: I2S substitutes space codepoint into input, blocking false branch; CMP_MAP gradient guides winner

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient walks the corpus toward the OLD_UYGHUR script-tag constant; I2S on top of the gradient dilutes/pollutes the corpus by substituting whole operands toward MONGOLIAN/NKO, starving the gradient that would reach OLD_UYGHUR.
one_line: CMP_MAP gradient reaches OLD_UYGHUR; I2S over-substitutes toward neighboring script tags

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient incrementally assembles the multi-consonant+joiner Indic syllable; adding I2S literal operand substitution on top disrupts that incremental assembly, displacing gradient-driven corpus growth and blocking the do-while loop-exit.
one_line: CMP_MAP gradient assembles Indic syllable; I2S literal substitution disrupts incremental assembly

mechanism_summary: I2S latches onto whichever neighboring Tirhuta codepoint was last logged (e.g. 0x11481), substituting that sibling value instead of the exact 0x1148B/0x1148D; havoc freely hits the exact 4-byte LE literal.
one_line: I2S overcommits to logged sibling Tirhuta codepoint; havoc hits exact 0x1148B/0x1148D literal

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient walks table-tag and script-tag CMPs incrementally toward the USE-routed script case; I2S on top over-commits the loser to high-frequency GSUB/GPOS literal constants, starving exploration of the less-frequent USE script case.
one_line: CMP_MAP gradient reaches USE script case; I2S overcommits to GSUB/GPOS tag constants

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP prefix-distance gradient ratchets the script-tag bytes toward the OLD_UYGHUR/line-380 case constants in short inputs; I2S on top diverts budget toward GSUB/GPOS table-tag comparisons, pulling the loser onto DFLT/latn and blocking the line-380 case.
one_line: CMP_MAP gradient reaches OLD_UYGHUR case; I2S diverts loser toward GSUB/GPOS table tags

mechanism_summary: I2S substitutes the switch comparand with exact case-literal codepoints (0x0020, 0x00A0, 0x2006), collapsing all inputs onto enumerated cases and starving the default arm; VP-only gradient survives without literal-snapping.
one_line: I2S snaps all inputs onto case literals, starving the switch default arm
